channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Morocco awarded Afcon title after CAF overturns result
item.description: Morocco declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations after the Confederation of African Football overturns the result of their final defeat to Senegal.

item.title: Bodo/Glimt's fairytale run comes to crashing halt
item.description: Bodo/Glimt's dream run in this season's Champions League may be over, but they bow out with their heads held high after making history.

item.title: I must have done things wrong if fans not happy - Slot
item.description: Head coach Arne Slot says he "must have done a lot of things wrong" for Liverpool fans to be "frustrated" at their recent form.

item.title: Chelsea's cheating - was a fine too lenient?
item.description: Sports editor Dan Roan analyses whether Chelsea's record fine for a raft of Premier League rule breaches was lenient.

item.title: Nonsense to say Spurs players don't care - Van de Ven
item.description: Tottenham defender Micky van de Ven says it is "the biggest nonsense" to suggest the club's players do not care about the team's current plight.

item.title: Fifa appears to rule out moving Iran's matches to Mexico
item.description: Fifa appears to rule out moving Iran's matches at this summer's World Cup to Mexico from the United States.

item.title: Why Bayern may have to play 16-year-old in goal
item.description: Bayern Munich may hand 16-year-old goalkeeper Leonard Prescott his first-team debut in the Champions League on Wednesday.

item.title: Wolves come back from two down to earn draw at Brentford
item.description: Tolu Arokodare scores shortly after coming on to earn Premier League bottom club Wolves a 2-2 draw at Brentford.

item.title: Chelsea fine 'meaningless if others aren't playing by same rules'
item.description: Rory Smith discusses whether Chelsea's fine will impact clubs if they "don't play by the same rules" in the Premier League.

item.title: 'Some players need to have a good look in the mirror'
item.description: Brentford boss Keith Andrews on his side's 2-2 draw with Wolves and the controversy surrounding Adam Armstrong's goal.

item.title: If we got away with it we'll take it - Wolves' Edwards
item.description: Wolves boss Rob Edwards praises his side for fighting back to draw at Brenford, amid controversy about a potential back-pass in the build-up to their first goal.

item.title: Richarlison's injury-time strike earns Spurs a point at Liverpool
item.description: Richarlison's 90th-minute equaliser earns Tottenham Hotspur a 1-1 draw at Liverpool to ease the pressure on interim coach Igor Tudor.

item.title: Wales leave captain Ramsey out of World Cup play-off squad
item.description: Captain Aaron Ramsey is left out of the Wales squad for this month's World Cup play-offs, casting further doubt over his future.

item.title: How Torpey aims to make Man Utd's academy 'best in the world'
item.description: New Man Utd academy chief Stephen Torpey talks for the first time about 'world class' demands of the job, not wanting the club to be parents, JJ Gabriel and Michael Carrick

item.title: How Chelsea signed a star team with hidden payments
item.description: Chelsea have been hit with a Premier League record fine of £10.75m and handed a suspended transfer ban. Here's what the written reasons said.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: Wealdstone unhappy as Hassan plays in Baller League
item.description: Wealdstone midfielder Sak Hassan is not expected to play for them again this season after appearing in the Baller League without permission, the club says.

item.title: VAR error cost Brighton penalty against Arsenal
item.description: Brighton should have been awarded a penalty in their 1-0 loss at home to Arsenal on 4 March, says the Premier League's KMI Panel.

item.title: Falkirk apologise to Dundee United for Sibbald scarf picture
item.description: Falkirk apologise to Dundee United over the way Craig Sibbald's pre-contract signing was announced, according to the Tannadice club.

item.title: From Bodo to Brentford - ranking this season's biggest overachievers
item.description: Bodo/Glimt must be the most romantic story in European football in decades but are they the team that has defied the odds the most this season? Here are all 10 contenders...

item.title: Arsenal trial and self-doubt: Valverde's journey to Real legend
item.description: Rejected by Arsenal as a teenager, Federico Valverde has transformed himself into a Real Madrid legend - and was the main man in their Champions League win over Manchester City last week.

item.title: Record-holder Dozzell on Dowman and the perils of early fame
item.description: Former Ipswich Town midfielder Jason Dozzell sings the praises of Arsenal's new kid on the block.

item.title: Bayern battle Man Utd for Anderson - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Bayern Munich rival Manchester United for Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson, Morgan Rogers could leave Aston Villa if they fail to make Champions League, Arsenal trio face uncertain future at the club, plus more.

item.title: Thiago and Rayan receive first Brazil call-ups
item.description: Brentford striker Igor Thiago and Bournemouth winger Rayan receive first call-ups to the Brazil squad for their friendlies with France and Croatia later this month.

item.title: 'We can't handcuff players to a decision' - O'Neill
item.description: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill says that Chris Atherton's switch to the Republic of Ireland is "disappointing, but we have to accept it".

item.title: Kerr winner sends Australia into Asian Cup final
item.description: Captain Sam Kerr scores the winner as Australia beat China 2-1 to reach the final of the Asian Cup.

item.title: Man Utd stay third with win against lacklustre Villa
item.description: Goals from Matheus Cunha and Benjamin Sesko help Manchester United fend off an attempted Aston Villa comeback, to win 3-1 at Old Trafford in the Premier League.

item.title: James stars as Chelsea beat Man Utd in Women's League Cup final
item.description: Lauren James gets on the scoresheet to put Chelsea on their way to a 2-0 win over Manchester United in the Women's League Cup Final at Ashton Gate.

item.title: Slot still searching for solution to conceding late goals
item.description: Liverpool head coach Arne Slot admits to not having the answers to his side's troubles conceding late goals this season.

item.title: Ten-man Leeds earn point at Palace after Calvert-Lewin penalty miss
item.description: Leeds United play out a goalless draw with Crystal Palace after Dominic Calvert-Lewin's missed first-half penalty is compounded by Gabriel Gudmundsson's sending off at Selhurst Park in the Premier League.

item.title: Forest earn point against Fulham to move out of relegation zone
item.description: Nottingham Forest play out a goalless draw with Fulham at the City Ground to move out of the bottom three of the Premier League.

item.title: 'Don't want to antagonise' - Chelsea may move controversial pre-match huddle
item.description: Chelsea expect to move their pre-match huddle away from the centre circle after referee Paul Tierney was caught in the middle on Saturday.

item.title: When could Wolves be relegated?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how soon Wolves can officially be relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: Who is 'leader' Morrison - the uncapped winger in NI squad?
item.description: Who is Kieran Morrison and what could he bring to the Northern Ireland team ahead of one of their biggest matches in recent years?

item.title: Mbappe back for Man City but Bellingham to sit out
item.description: Real Madrid striker Kylian Mbappe is fit to face Manchester City in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tie but midfielder Jude Bellingham will not feature despite travelling over the match.

item.title: Scotland boss Clarke 'hasn't had contract offer'
item.description: National coach Steve Clarke says the Scottish FA "haven't put an offer in front of me" as he nears the end of his current deal.

item.title: The key takeaways from Scotland's March squad
item.description: BBC Scotland looks at the key takeaways from Steve Clarke's Scotland squad to face Japan and Ivory Coast in preparation for the World Cup finals.

item.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
item.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

item.title: Chelsea's James could miss weeks with injury as Neto escapes ban
item.description: Chelsea defender Reece James could be out for weeks with a hamstring injury, says head coach Liam Rosenior.

item.title: Fernandes is a joy to watch - Rooney
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Wayne Rooney analyses Bruno Fernandes' crucial role in Manchester United's 3-1 win over Aston Villa at Old Trafford in the Premier League.

item.title: 'A moment he'll never forget' - Rooney on Dowman goal
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Wayne Rooney describes Max Dowman becoming the youngest goalscorer in Premier League history as a "moment he'll never forget" and argues it will bring those connected to Arsenal closer together, seeing an academy graduate make his mark on the first team.

item.title: How Spurs going direct changed the game and caused Liverpool problems
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Micah Richards analyses how Spurs' decision to switch from playing out the back in favour of going long caused Liverpool "big problems" in their 1-1 draw in the Premier League at Anfield.

item.title: Spurs 'happy' to earn a 'very good point' at Liverpool - Tudor
item.description: Tottenham manager Igor Tudor believes his side tried "until the last second" to earn a 1-1 draw against Liverpool in the Premier League at Anfield.

item.title: Ngumoha a 'constant threat' in first Premier League start - Slot
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot believes Rio Ngumoha's performance on his first Premier League start was "one of the positives" in a 1-1 draw with Tottenham - a result he describes as "two points dropped".

item.title: Casemiro and Fernandes connection 'can't be coached' - Carrick
item.description: Manchester United manager Michael Carrick praises the "great" connection between Casemiro and Bruno Fernandes, after the duo combined for the opening goal in their 3-1 victory over Aston Villa.

item.title: Dowman has aura, belief, and GCSEs to finish - Rooney
item.description: Ex-England striker Wayne Rooney praises Max Dowman's record-breaking impact for Arsenal - but wonders if the 16-year-old might have to focus on his GCSEs for the rest of the season.

item.title: Spurs answer Tudor's call with show of fight in the face of crisis
item.description: Spurs interim coach Igor Tudor told his players they could "cry or fight". BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty saw a display of defiance to draw at Liverpool.

item.title: Fifa sanctions Nigeria and DR Congo over World Cup play-off
item.description: Fifa fines the Nigeria Football Federation and DR Congo Football Association for separate disciplinary breaches by supporters.

item.title: Carrick continues to make case for permanent Man Utd role
item.description: Wayne Rooney calls on Manchester United to give Michael Carrick the manager's job after boosting their hopes of Champions League qualification.

item.title: Have Old Firm wrestled title momentum away from Hearts?
item.description: After wins for Celtic and Rangers and a defeat for leaders Hearts, what next in the captivating Scottish Premiership title race?

item.title: Fan finally shaves beard after Oxford awarded first penalty in 675 days
item.description: Oxford United fan Gary Hudson had refused to shave until his team were awarded a spot kick.

item.title: Gudmundsson sending off a 'clear mistake' - Farke
item.description: Leeds manager Daniel Farke describes left-back Gabriel Gudmundsson's sending off for a second bookable offence, a "clear mistake" in Leeds United's 0-0 draw away to Crystal Palace.

item.title: Fulham lack 'sharpness' in decision making - Silva on Forest draw
item.description: Marco Silva says his side were wasteful in the final third but he remains happy to keep a clean sheet, as Fulham earned themselves a 0-0 draw at Nottingham Forest.

item.title: James is the best in the world in terms of talent - Williams
item.description: BBC Sports pundits Alex Scott, Ellen White, Steph Houghton and Fara Williams discuss what it will take for Lauren James to become the best in the world.

item.title: League Cup final win has to give us belief - Cuthbert
item.description: Erin Cuthbert said captaining Chelsea in the League Cup final was "special" after their 2–0 win over Manchester United at Ashton Gate.

item.title: 'Nervous' Palace 'rush' search of winning goal - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says his side are "losing our structure" going forward and need to stay "calm" pursuing a winning goal, as the Eagles play out a goalless draw with Leeds United at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Villa unable to overcome 'huge challenge' of Man Utd - Emery
item.description: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery admits his side were unable to "control" enough possession of the ball to contain Manchester United as Villa suffer a 3-1 defeat at Old Trafford.

item.title: Quiz: Who does Dowman join as Premier League's 10 youngest scorers?
item.description: Arsenal's Max Dowman became the youngest ever player to score a Premier League goal. But who else is on the list?

item.title: 'They know where I am' - could Redknapp return to manage Spurs?
item.description: Former Tottenham boss Harry Redknapp says the Spurs hierarchy "know where I am" if they decide to replace interim boss Igor Tudor – so could the 79-year-old make a sensational return to management?

item.title: Brentford, the World Cup and an itch to return to Gaelic football
item.description: Brentford and Republic of Ireland captain Nathan Collins tells Kelly Somers about his dreams of the World Cup.

item.title: The deal that cost father and son's lives in 'forgotten disaster'
item.description: A family remembers how their loved ones never returned from the fatal 1946 stadium crush in Bolton.

item.title: Too good to go down? Ranking shock Premier League relegations
item.description: Premier League champions, FA Cup winners and club legends have all suffered the unwanted fate, but which relegated side really was "too good to go down"?

item.title: From Norfolk to La Masia - Tavares is England's one to watch
item.description: Family, ex-coaches and former team-mates of Ajay Tavares describe the 16-year-old's rise from a small Norfolk market town to FC Barcelona.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: What is St Totteringham's Day and when could it happen?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what St Totteringham's Day is and what its origins are

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: 'I don't regret anything' - Martin on Rangers reign
item.description: Russell Martin holds no regrets from his turbulent spell as Rangers head coach and says abuse he received was difficult on those close to him.

item.title: Man United's Fletcher earns Scotland Under-21 call
item.description: Tyler Fletcher, son of former Scotland captain Darren, has been called up to the under-21 squad ahead of games against Czech Republic and Portugal.

item.title: Football Ultras: A menace or a force for good?
item.description: Smoke bombs, pyrotechnics, balaclavas and punch-ups - welcome to the world of the football Ultras.

item.title: Teenager Curtis given maiden Scotland call-up
item.description: Rangers' on-loan Kilmarnock winger Findlay Curtis is a surprise inclusion in Steve Clarke's Scotland squad for two friendly matches later this month.

item.title: Humble beginnings, Ronaldo & NBA - how Kvaratskhelia became a star
item.description: Guillem Balague analyses the rise of Paris St-Germain's Georgian superstar Khvicha Kvaratskhelia.

item.title: Masked fan unplugs VAR monitor in German second division match
item.description: A masked fan unplugged a VAR monitor in a German second division match as the referee went to the pitchside screen to check a penalty award.

item.title: Quiz: One question for each team in the Champions League last 16
item.description: The Champions League is back, but how much do you know about each of the 16 teams still in the competition?

item.title: Serial winners Chelsea block out noise to triumph again
item.description: No matter what form Chelsea are in, winning trophies feels inevitable - and they did it again in the Women's League Cup.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
